evidence_quotes.quote: Now a days, games are only sold in digital form, or in an ability to buy a box that has a digital code for it, yet the asking price is the same or more as it would be in physical form, with full control for games publishers or platform holders to revoke,remove or delete our ownership of these products that are sold to us with the same wording as it was 30 years ago, without the same rights to said products.
If games are explicitly sold as licenses or subscriptions they should clearly state their end date.

paragraphs.text: Greetings! I wish to express my concerns and suggestions why game ownership and preservation should be included in this Act. Games have changed in the last 30 years, from products that were sold in CD's or cartridges, that when bought would remain in buyers ownership until the physical media it was stored on would break or be destroyed by the owner of the product or by circumstances of life. Now a days, games are only sold in digital form, or in an ability to buy a box that has a digital code for it, yet the asking price is the same or more as it would be in physical form, with full control for games publishers or platform holders to revoke,remove or delete our ownership of these products that are sold to us with the same wording as it was 30 years ago, without the same rights to said products. There are countless examples of media and games being destroyed without repercussion or an ability still use said products that consumers bought because games industry leaders like EA, Activision/Blizzard, Microsoft, Sony, Nintendo, Ubisoft do not feel like consumers should be able to enjoy or use products that We as consumers have bought and paid for because they say so. We are sold a product, but the actions of these companies, act as laws and obligation only apply to their ability to take our money, not to provide a product fairly. Yet games offer you more methods to spend money on them, there endless supply of ways to provide these companies with more money, yet there Seems to be no protections, no insensitive, no rules for these companies to provide, guarantee or protect the products to consumers. This wiki page lists an incomplete list of games that are either destroyed completely without an ability to play or use products paid for, at risk of being destroyed, maintained by third party means, maintained by developers themselves. https://stopkillinggames.wiki.gg/wiki/Dead_game_list My request as EU citizen, that believes in our rights and our consumer laws, as digital space should not be a free domain for exploitation by giant companies that make Billions selling products globally, easier and faster than ever before. It should also be a place where our rights to products we buy should matter, should be respected and followed by the industry and protected by EU. Games that require online services to function that the publisher does not want to maintain anymore should have an end of life plan how to provide the product to customers to use without said online services provided by publisher. Games that do NOT require online services to run, should not be removed,deleted or destroyed from users digital libraries, and if not possible to maintain the existence in digital libraries should provide an official way to download and install said product, this is not a request from developers to maintain active version. Just a version of the game before its end of support. Games that have micro-transactions should guarantee that a product bought inside the games stores, is guaranteed to be usable or provided, even in the games end of support time. If games are explicitly sold as licenses or subscriptions they should clearly state their end date.I do not believe that any games should be destroyed or cannot be preserved, however, at the bare minimum the industry should stop being allowed to lie to consumers about what they are buying. In digital age, we cannot forget about consumers, because if there is no ownership, if there are no fair laws for consumers, no protections, there no reasons to participate in the digital consumerism, which in turn makes piracy and illegal means more attractive which, damages industry, economy and the hobby itself.
